title: Intermittent duplicate charges under load (payments-service)
symptom: A fraction of checkouts charge the customer twice; the second PSP charge posts ~40ms after the first and carries the SAME client-supplied Idempotency-Key. Idempotency should make the retried POST /charges a no-op returning the original charge.
business_impact: Customer money double-debited; 3 customer tickets; on-call paged.
log_excerpt: app/payments/idempotency.py:48  cache MISS for ik_3af91c -> proceeding to charge   (logged by BOTH requests)
repro_signal: Concurrency-dependent. Serial or >250ms apart: never duplicates. Two same-key requests within ~50ms: duplicates; higher concurrency -> higher rate (~0.3% at spike, 0 at low traffic). Mobile client retries on a 2s timeout while the first request is still in flight.
constraints: retried request must return the ORIGINAL charge
fix must hold under concurrency, not just the happy path
key_observation: BOTH concurrent requests log a cache MISS for the same key before either records a charge -> smells like check-then-act with no per-key mutual exclusion.
source: systematic-debugging Phase 1.1 (read the error carefully); Iron Law holds at entry (no fix yet)
